projects.name: Tond Tenga
projects.description: The first project globally to register under an ICVCM-approved VCS methodology. Restoring 12,000 ha of degraded land across four regions of Burkina Faso through native tree planting and agroforestry, in partnership with 185 villages. All carbon revenues flow directly to communities. Part of the Great Green Wall initiative. 40-year project projecting 3.7M tCO2e removal.

projects.name: Mikoko Pamoja
projects.description: The first blue carbon project in the world to fund conservation through carbon credits. A community-led mangrove conservation and restoration initiative in Gazi Bay on the south coast of Kenya, protecting 117 ha of mangrove forest. Revenue funds clean water, school construction, and school books. UN Equator Prize winner 2017. Certified under Plan Vivo, not Verra.

projects.name: Save the Sand
projects.description: A community co-owned afforestation project in the Sabie-Sand River Catchment in Mpumalanga, restoring 40,000 ha of degraded land through native tree planting and agroforestry. Structured as a carbon pre-sale rather than grant-funded, with a four-tier community benefit-sharing structure. An Article 6.2 ITMO pathway is in progress, positioning the project for compliance market access.

projects.developer: Carbon Green Investments (project owner) - South Pole (former carbon asset developer, terminated 2023)
projects.status_note: Withdrawn from the Verra registry in May 2024 by project owner Carbon Green Investments following a Verra investigation that found 57% of the 27M credits issued were excess and did not correspond to actual emission reductions. South Pole, the carbon asset developer, terminated its involvement in October 2023. The project represents the largest integrity failure in the voluntary carbon market to date and is retained here as a reference case.
